name: AWS CLI
description: Official command-line interface for Amazon Web Services — manage EC2, S3, Lambda, ECS, and 200+ AWS services from the terminal
when_to_use: Managing AWS resources, deploying to AWS, scripting cloud operations, configuring IAM and networking

install.command: brew install awscli || curl 'https://awscli.amazonaws.com/awscli-exe-linux-x86_64.zip' -o awscli.zip && unzip awscli.zip && sudo ./aws/install

name: Google Cloud CLI
description: Official CLI for Google Cloud Platform — manage Compute Engine, Cloud Run, GKE, BigQuery, and all GCP services
when_to_use: Managing GCP resources, deploying to Cloud Run or GKE, managing IAM, querying BigQuery

install.command: brew install --cask google-cloud-sdk || curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

name: Azure CLI
description: Official CLI for Microsoft Azure — manage VMs, App Service, AKS, Azure Functions, and all Azure services from the terminal
when_to_use: Managing Azure resources, deploying to Azure App Service or AKS, configuring Azure AD and networking

install.command: brew install azure-cli || curl -sL https://aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb | sudo bash

name: Terraform
description: Infrastructure as Code tool by HashiCorp — define cloud resources in HCL, plan changes, and apply them across any cloud provider
when_to_use: Provisioning and managing cloud infrastructure declaratively, multi-cloud deployments, infrastructure drift detection

install.command: brew install terraform || curl -fsSL https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform/1.x/terraform_1.x_linux_amd64.zip -o tf.zip && unzip tf.zip

name: Pulumi
description: Infrastructure as Code using real programming languages — define cloud resources in TypeScript, Python, Go, or Java instead of DSLs
when_to_use: IaC with familiar programming languages, when you need loops/conditionals/abstractions that HCL makes difficult

install.command: brew install pulumi || curl -fsSL https://get.pulumi.com | sh

name: kubectl
description: Kubernetes command-line tool — manage clusters, deploy applications, inspect resources, and debug pods across any Kubernetes environment
when_to_use: Managing Kubernetes clusters, deploying and scaling applications, debugging pods, inspecting cluster state

name: Helm
description: Kubernetes package manager — install, upgrade, and manage applications on Kubernetes using reusable chart templates
when_to_use: Deploying complex applications to Kubernetes, managing release versions, using community charts for common services

name: Kustomize
description: Kubernetes configuration customization tool — overlay-based approach to managing environment-specific YAML without templates
when_to_use: Managing Kubernetes manifests across environments (dev/staging/prod) without templating, patching base configs with overlays
